zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Collier於1453年出生在法國帕-德-卡拉斯時，他的父親Robert Collier I28歲，母親Agnes Venables20歲。他於大約1483年與Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children )在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1505年在英國斯塔福爾史東享年52歲，埋葬在英國戴文米亞福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Collier于1453年出生在法国帕-德-卡拉斯时，他的父亲Robert Collier I28岁，母亲Agnes Venables20岁。他于大约1483年与Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children )在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1505年在英国斯塔福爾史東享年52岁，埋葬在英国戴文米亞福。

nameMeanings.name: Collier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English colier, in most parts of the country ‘maker or seller of charcoal’, but in some areas (such as Bolton le Moors and Wigan, Lancashire) where coal measures were near the surface, ‘miner or seller of coal’ (in the modern sense, ‘fossil fuel’). The name was taken to Ireland from England and was first recorded there in 1305. In Petty's ‘census’ of 1659, it was recorded as a principal surname in Meath.
English: occupational name from Middle English coilour, coliour, culliour, Old French coileor, coillour ‘tax collector’. Surnames with this origin seem to have died out in Britain.
French (northern): from collier ‘collar’, a metonymic occupational name for a maker of collars.

variants.name: Callier
meanings: French: from an Old French term denoting earthenware and, by extension, a particular type of earthenware vessel from which new wine was drunk, hence probably a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such crocks.
French: from caielier, an occupational name for a chair maker, from an agent derivative of caiel ‘chair’.

English (Staffordshire): probably a variant of the occupational name Collier ‘charcoal burner’, though in some cases perhaps a variant of Caller, from a Middle English side-form callier with substitution of the suffix -ier for -er. In Kent and Somerset, Caller is chiefly a variant of the nickname or habitational name Callow . Elsewhere it may derive either from Middle English caller ‘maker of cauls’ (baskets, nets, women's close-fitting caps or headdresses for covering tied up hair), from Middle English calle ‘basket, net’ (Old English cawl), or from a topographic or occupational name for someone who lived by or worked at a sheep pen or pigsty (Middle English calle), synonymous with atte Calle. See Call .

variants.name: Coller
meanings: Altered form of Swiss German and German   Koller   or   Kohler  .

variants.name: Collar
meanings: Spanish: from collar ‘collar’. This name is found especially in Asturias and Extremadura.
Americanized form of German   Koller   or   Kohler  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Collier was born in 1453, in Pas-de-Calais, France, his father, Robert Collier I, was 28 and his mother, Agnes Venables, was 20. He married Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) about 1483, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1505, in Stone, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 52, and was buried in Meavy, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Collier est né en 1453, à Pas-de-Calais, France, son père, Robert Collier I, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Agnes Venables, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) environ 1483, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1505, à Stone, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Meavy, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Collier 1453 in Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Collier I 28 und seine Mutter Agnes Venables 20. Er hat um 1483 in England, United Kingdom Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1505 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Stone, Staffordshire, England und wurde in Meavy, Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Collier è nato il nel 1453 nel Pas-de-Calais, Francia, il suo padre, Robert Collier I, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Venables, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) circa 1483, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1505, nel Stone, Staffordshire, England, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolto a Meavy, Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Collierが1453年にフランスプアス-デ-カレイスで生まれた時，父Robert Collier Iは28歳，母Agnes Venablesは20歳であった。約1483年にイギリスイギリスでIsabella Doddington ( 6 Children )と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1505年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアストーンで52歳で死去し，イギリスデボンメアビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Collier(이)가 1453년 프랑스 파스-데-캘레이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Collier I(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Venables(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1483년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children )(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1505년 영국 스태퍼드실르 스톤에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 미비에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Collier nasceu em 1453, em Pas-de-Calais, França, seu pai, Robert Collier I, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Venables, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) aproximadamente 1483, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1505, em Stone, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Meavy, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Collier родился в 1453 г. в местности пас-де-калеи, Франция, его отцу по имени Robert Collier I было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Venables, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) около 1483 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1505 г. в местности стоун, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности миви, девон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Collier nació en 1453, en Pas-de-Calais, Francia, su papá, Robert Collier I, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Agnes Venables, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) aproximadamente 1483, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1505, en Stone, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Meavy, Devon, Inglaterra.
